paragraph_text: By the first Monday after launch, Sam Altman posted that ChatGPT had crossed one million users; the word users did not say whether those were monthly actives, registered accounts, or repeat visitors. Two months later, Reuters, citing a UBS study that drew on Similarweb data, reported a different kind of measure: an estimate of 100 million monthly active users in January 2023, with about 13 million daily unique visitors on average. That was not OpenAI telemetry, and it was not the same unit as Altman's launch-week milestone. It was still the signal that mattered to the industry: whatever ChatGPT was, people were not merely trying it once and leaving.
allowed_use: May be used as a candidate Chapter 7 adoption paragraph if source IDs stay attached and metric labels remain visible.
prohibited_reading: Do not call ChatGPT the fastest-growing app in history, do not merge users/MAU/daily unique visitors, and do not imply OpenAI confirmed the Reuters/UBS/Similarweb estimates.

paragraph_text: The paragraph deliberately omits the fastest-growing consumer-app superlative because the UBS note remains uncaptured and the comparison would become too headline-like without same-scope source evidence.
allowed_use: Use as a negative-control note beside any later adoption prose.
prohibited_reading: Do not revive the superlative in headings, captions, pull quotes, or narration unless a later pass captures the UBS note or comparable same-scope data.

paragraph_text: The paragraph contains three explicit metric firewalls: users are undefined, MAU is Reuters/UBS/Similarweb-attributed, and daily unique visitors are a traffic measure rather than MAU or registrations.
allowed_use: Use as the minimum standard for any live Chapter 7 adoption prose.
prohibited_reading: Do not shorten the paragraph in a way that drops attribution or turns estimates into narrator-certified facts.
